title: Stop and Remove a Container
description:
## Clean Up After Yourself

A leftover container named **`stale`** is running and needs to go.

**Your task:** **stop** and **remove** the `stale` container so it no longer appears in `docker ps -a`.

> 💡 Stop it with `docker stop stale`, then remove it with `docker rm stale`. (Or do both at once with `docker rm -f stale`.)

hints.title: Stop then remove
hints.body: Stopping ends the process; removing deletes the container record.
hints.command: docker stop stale && docker rm stale

hints.title: Force-remove in one step
hints.body: docker rm -f stops and removes a running container in a single command.

validation.description: Checks that no container named 'stale' remains (running or stopped).
